attacking_style: Not a natural attacker; operates primarily as a sprinter but has shown capacity to finish from breakaway chase groups on selective terrain, suggesting emerging versatility beyond pure flat finishes.
team_tactics: Relies on leadout train positioning in sprint stages, though team execution has shown vulnerability at intermediate sprint points where aggressive positioning led to crashes affecting both sprinter and leadout.
terrain_preference: Reduced bunch sprints and selective mid-mountain stages where pure climbers are dropped but gradients favor resilient sprinter types. Flat pure sprints expose positioning weaknesses.
vulnerability: Positioning discipline at intermediate sprints (crashed twice in observations); struggles with final positioning against top-tier sprinters in large bunch kicks, often finishing on podium but unable to win.
key_patterns: Demonstrates better climbing range than traditional sprinter profile allows (breakaway chase finish, reduced sprints)
Intermediate sprint aggression creates crash risk; two incidents observed removing both rider and leadout
Podium finisher rather than winner in reduced sprints; positioning deficit in final 200m documented
form_trajectory: positive: 2021 showed expanded range with second place from breakaway group, suggesting evolution beyond pure sprinter limitations seen in 2019-2020
